rationale: The citing paper models consumers’ latent path-to-purchase states and channel visits (including paid/display-related touchpoints), while the cited paper is a stage-based purchase-funnel study of display ad effects, so there is clear conceptual overlap on funnel-state heterogeneity and channel response. Given this plausible role as prior funnel/mechanism evidence (not a clearly incidental citation), and only sparse reference-context evidence beyond the DOI match, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
